conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier: a conductor with a resistance R=15Ω has a current I=2A passing through it, how much heat Q is generated by this conductor in t=10s?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned earlier, given resistance, current, and time, find Joule heat</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:57] Time: 2025-07-27-10:40, Content: The user explained to the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the form of Joule's law is Q = I × R × t^2. This formula can be used to solve for the generated heat Q when the current I, resistance R, and time t are known. Low relevance memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 1 relevant entry, [mem-id:57] gives Q = I × R × t². Given I=2A, R=15Ω, t=10s, then t²=100, Q=2×15×100=3000 J.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the world of Azeroth, the generated heat Q = 3000 J.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
